models.generation_context: 6th-gen 4Runner, all-new for 2025 MY on the TNGA-F body-on-frame platform shared with Tacoma. Hybrid i-FORCE MAX option new.
models.model_summary: Toyota's body-on-frame midsize SUV is offered in nine trims spanning two powertrain lines: i-FORCE (2.4L turbo, 278 hp, RWD or 4WD) and i-FORCE MAX (2.4L turbo-hybrid, 326 hp / 465 lb-ft, 4WD-standard). Trims from urban-luxury Limited to overlanding Trailhunter and TRD Pro share the new platform but differ widely in ride height, suspension and equipment. Tow rating is up to 6,000 lb.

reliability.summary: All-new for 2025; long-term reliability data isn't yet available. CR's early predicted-reliability score for the 6th-gen is below average due to new powertrain and infotainment. Toyota's body-on-frame trucks historically rate well after first-year sorting.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The redesigned 6th-gen 4Runner is too new to have an APEAL track record. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers welcome the new platform's modern ergonomics, more comfortable on-road manners, and i-FORCE MAX's strong low-end torque, while noting that the turbo-four can feel coarse and the heavily-equipped TRD Pro / Trailhunter pricing rivals luxury SUVs. Off-road bona fides are strong on TRD Off-Road and above; the Trailhunter's factory ARB and Old Man Emu kit draws particular praise.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners like the modern cabin, hybrid torque, and rugged styling but complain about turbo-four noise, mileage shortfall vs EPA, and steep pricing on top trims. A handful of early-build infotainment glitches reported.

models.generation_context: Refreshed for 2026 — Toyota dropped the bZ4X name and significantly upgraded the EV: larger 74.7 kWh battery option, more power, NACS port standard, new front-end styling.
models.model_summary: The bZ is Toyota's compact electric crossover, redone for 2026 with a bigger 74.7-kWh battery option (314 mi EPA on XLE Plus FWD), higher outputs (up to 338 hp AWD), and a NACS Tesla-Supercharger-native fast-charge port. Five trims span XLE (FWD, FWD Plus, AWD) and Limited (FWD, AWD).

reliability.summary: First Toyota-badged EV generation; 2026 introduces a substantially revised drivetrain and battery so prior bZ4X data isn't directly transferable. CR is provisionally average. No JD Power VDS yet.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The rebranded bZ has no separate APEAL history. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the 2026 bZ's larger battery, real-world 300+ mile range on FWD Plus trims, NACS charging access to Tesla Superchargers, and quicker AWD dual-motor performance. Critiques remain on the awkward fender cladding styling, modest 150-kW DC peak (vs. 250+ kW on rivals), and cabin materials that lag the price.

owner_reviews.summary: Too few owner reviews available for the 2026 refresh; predecessor bZ4X owners praised quiet cabin and ride but flagged range and slow DC fast charging.

models.generation_context: All-new for 2026 as a rugged outdoor-styled variant of the bZ EV platform. Arrives at dealers March 2026.
models.model_summary: The bZ Woodland is a rugged, AWD-standard EV crossover that shares the bZ's 74.7 kWh battery and NACS port but adds 6 inches of length, 8.4 in ground clearance, roof rails and a more powerful 375-hp dual-motor system. Offered in two trims (bZ Woodland and Woodland Premium); EPA range is 281 mi standard, 260 mi with optional all-terrain tires.

reliability.summary: Brand-new nameplate for 2026 — no JD Power VDS coverage (VDS measures 3-year-old vehicles) and no Consumer Reports model-specific predicted reliability score. Toyota brand 2026 VDS PP100: 185 (top-5 mass market). CR 2026 Annual Auto Reliability Survey placed Toyota #1 in reliability-only ranking with a brand score of 62 (Subaru 68, Lexus 65, then Toyota at 62 per CR Watchdog; CR's media room places Toyota at the top with improvements in Tundra, Tacoma, and 4Runner driving the brand back to #1).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The bZ Woodland is a brand-new nameplate with no APEAL history. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Initial press impressions praise the Woodland's higher seating position, rugged styling, strong 375-hp dual-motor performance and NACS standard charging, with 0-60 quoted at 4.4 sec. Reservations involve a relatively modest 150-kW DC peak and the EV range hit when fitted with all-terrain tires (260 mi vs 281 mi).

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews not yet available — vehicle launched March 2026.

models.generation_context: All-new for 2026 as a battery-electric compact crossover replacing the discontinued ICE C-HR. Built on Toyota's e-TNGA platform with NACS port standard.
models.model_summary: The 2026 C-HR is a brand-new EV-only compact crossover with standard AWD and a 338-hp dual-motor system. Two trims (SE and XSE) share the 74.7 kWh battery; EPA range is 287 mi on SE with 18-inch wheels and 273 mi on XSE with 20-inch wheels. Standard NACS port gives native Tesla Supercharger access.

reliability.summary: All-new EV C-HR for 2026 — no JD Power VDS coverage (VDS measures 3-year-old vehicles) and no Consumer Reports model-specific predicted reliability score. Toyota brand 2026 VDS PP100: 185 (top-5 mass market). CR 2026 Annual Auto Reliability Survey placed Toyota #1 in reliability-only ranking (brand score 62). Shares powertrain/battery architecture with revised bZ.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). All-new EV C-HR has no APEAL history. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Early reviewers praise the EV C-HR's bold styling, strong 4.9-sec 0-60, standard dual-motor AWD, and NACS access at a competitive price ($37,000 base). Reservations: rear-seat space is tight under the sloping roof, DC fast charging caps at 150 kW (below class leaders), and there is no FWD option for a longer-range entry trim.

models.generation_context: 9th-gen (XV80), introduced for 2025 MY; hybrid-only from launch. 2026 adds Nightshade Edition.
models.model_summary: The Camry is Toyota's flagship midsize sedan, fully hybrid since the 2025 redesign and offered in five grades (LE, SE, Nightshade, XLE, XSE) with optional electronic on-demand AWD. A 2.5L 4-cylinder paired with Toyota's fifth-generation hybrid system delivers 225 hp FWD or 232 hp AWD, with EPA ratings up to 51 combined MPG on LE FWD. Toyota Safety Sense 3.0 is standard on every trim.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS (Feb 2026) named the Toyota Camry the highest-ranked midsize sedan — a Model Award winner. Toyota brand 2026 VDS PP100: 185 (top-5 mass market). Consumer Reports 2026 Annual Auto Reliability Survey: CR called out the Camry as bouncing back from a redesigned-MY dip, with the 2025/26 Camry posting a reliability score of 74 — tied with Honda Accord for the second-best in the midsize-sedan category. Toyota brand reliability score in 2026: 62 (#1 in reliability-only ranking).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The prior generation generally scored above the midsize-sedan segment average. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Edmunds rates the 2026 Camry 8.3/10 and named it a Top Rated sedan two years running, praising fuel economy, interior space, standard driver aids, and value. Critics agree the ride is composed and the hybrid system is smooth and responsive, but interior materials and the touchscreen UI draw consistent complaints. MotorTrend recommends it as a sensible daily but flags weak cabin build quality compared to similarly priced rivals.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the smooth hybrid drivetrain, real-world fuel economy near EPA estimates, comfortable ride, and roomy interior. Recurring complaints involve laggy/buggy multimedia software, seat comfort on longer drives, and interior plastics that don't match the price.

models.generation_context: 12th-gen (E210), launched 2019; mid-cycle refresh for 2023. 2026 standardizes Blind Spot Monitor and Rear Cross-Traffic Alert across all grades.
models.model_summary: Toyota's compact sedan is offered in three gas trims (LE, SE, XSE) with a 169-hp 2.0L four-cylinder and CVT, and in hybrid LE/SE/XLE trims with a 138-hp 1.8L hybrid system and optional AWD on LE and SE. Toyota Safety Sense 3.0 plus BSM/RCTA are standard across the lineup for 2026.

reliability.summary: Current-gen Corolla has historically posted above-average Consumer Reports predicted reliability; JD Power has not published a 2026-specific VDS yet. The platform is well-established.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Predecessor years for current-gen Corolla scored near segment average. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently call the Corolla an unflashy but well-rounded compact sedan — fuel-efficient, easy to drive, and stuffed with standard safety tech. Critics note that interior plastics feel cheap, rear seat space is tight, and the 169-hp 2.0L can feel coarse at full throttle. The hybrid is widely viewed as the better daily-driver pick if budget allows.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners highlight reliability, real-world fuel economy, and low operating costs. Common complaints: road noise, modest power, and small back-seat foot room.

models.generation_context: First-gen Corolla Cross launched for 2022 MY; mid-cycle refresh for 2026 with new front-end styling differentiating gas (rugged grille) from hybrid (color-matched grille) variants. Assembled in Huntsville, Alabama.
models.model_summary: Toyota's subcompact crossover offers two parallel powertrain lines: a 169-hp 2.0L gas four (L/LE/XLE; FWD or AWD) and a 196-hp 2.0L hybrid system (S/SE/XSE; AWD standard). EPA combined is 32 MPG (gas FWD) or 42 MPG (hybrid AWD).

reliability.summary: Mature TNGA-C-based platform; CR predicted reliability is favorable. No JD Power VDS yet for 2026 specifically.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published 2026 APEAL; prior years are average for the subcompact-SUV class.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers see the Corolla Cross as a competent, easy-to-park entry crossover that is most appealing in hybrid form (196 hp, 42 MPG, AWD standard). Criticism centers on the gas model's slow acceleration, modest highway power, and basic interior materials at the price.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners like the value, fuel economy and standard safety tech; common complaints involve noisy 2.0L engine on gas trims and tight rear seat for adults.

models.model_summary: Toyota's compact hatchback shares its 2.0L 169-hp four-cylinder and CVT with the Corolla sedan but offers a more practical liftback body with 17.8 cu ft of cargo behind the rear seat. Three trims are offered: SE, the new heritage-styled FX Edition, and XSE; all are FWD only and ICE only — no hybrid hatchback is sold in the US.

reliability.summary: Shares the Corolla sedan's mature E210 platform and 2.0L engine, both with a strong long-term reliability track record. JD Power VDS not yet published for 2026 specifically.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Corolla Hatchback as a competent, frugal compact with sharper handling than the sedan and useful cargo flexibility, but knock the noisy 2.0L engine at full throttle, the cheap-feeling interior, and the lack of a hybrid option in the US. The new FX Edition gets attention for its retro hot-hatch graphics, not extra performance.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners like the cargo flexibility, fuel economy, and standard safety tech; complaints center on engine noise, interior plastics, and tight rear-seat space for adults.

models.generation_context: First US Crown sedan generation (XG30), launched for 2023 MY as a tall, AWD-only hybrid sedan replacing the Avalon. 2026 adds the Nightshade trim.
models.model_summary: The Crown is Toyota's flagship hybrid sedan — a tall, AWD-only four-door offered in three Toyota Hybrid System grades (XLE, Limited, Nightshade) producing 236 hp combined, plus a top Platinum grade using the Hybrid MAX 2.4L turbo system for 340 hp. EPA combined ranges from 41 MPG on the 2.5L hybrids to 30 MPG on the Hybrid MAX Platinum.

reliability.summary: Crown is in its first US generation; JD Power VDS not yet published for 2026. CR has flagged early-build concerns on infotainment and electronics, partly offset by mature Toyota hybrid components.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Crown's quiet, premium cabin, smooth standard-hybrid drivetrain, and standout Hybrid MAX performance on the Platinum, but knock the awkward proportions, firm Platinum ride, and Lexus-adjacent pricing. Most see it as a niche pick for buyers wanting a hybrid sedan with SUV-style ride height; the Hybrid MAX Platinum draws the most enthusiast attention.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners highlight the quiet ride, standard AWD, and excellent 41 MPG combined on the 2.5L hybrid; complaints focus on small trunk for the size, infotainment quirks, and limited rear headroom from the sloping roof.

models.generation_context: Launched for 2025 MY as a hybrid wagon-style crossover slotting between Crown sedan and Highlander. 2026 adds two-tone paint options.
models.model_summary: The Crown Signia is a hybrid-only, AWD-standard two-row wagon offered in XLE and Limited grades. It pairs a 2.5L four-cylinder with Toyota's hybrid system for 240 net combined horsepower and an EPA-rated 38 MPG combined, with 25 cu ft behind the rear seats and up to 68.8 cu ft with the rear seats folded.

reliability.summary: Shares the well-established 2.5L Toyota Hybrid System with Camry, RAV4 and Crown sedan; CR's early predicted-reliability score is favorable. JD Power VDS not yet published.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The still-new Crown Signia nameplate has no APEAL track record yet. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Crown Signia as a quiet, comfortable, premium-feeling alternative to a Highlander for buyers who prefer wagon proportions over SUV height. Praise centers on ride comfort, real-world fuel economy and standard AWD; complaints are limited 240-hp acceleration, no PHEV option, and a higher starting price than a Venza or RAV4 Hybrid Limited.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the smooth hybrid drivetrain, fuel economy, cargo space, and quiet cabin; common complaints involve modest acceleration and tight rear headroom for taller passengers under the sloping roof.

models.generation_context: First-gen GR Corolla, launched for 2023 MY. Gazoo Racing's halo Corolla — separate model from the standard Corolla per master spec's performance-variant rule.
models.model_summary: The GR Corolla is Toyota Gazoo Racing's high-performance compact hatch — a 300-hp, 295-lb-ft 1.6L turbo three-cylinder mated to either a 6-speed manual with rev-matching or an 8-speed automatic, driving all four wheels through a GR-FOUR system with Torsen LSDs at both axles. Two trims (Base and Premium Plus) for 2026, both with extensive chassis stiffening enhancements over the prior model.

reliability.summary: Four model years of production; reliability is average — turbocharger inspections and recalls earlier in the run; mature now. Limited production volumes constrain dataset.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The GR Corolla is low-volume and unlikely to receive a separate APEAL ranking even when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Enthusiast press is uniformly enthusiastic about the GR Corolla's chassis balance, manual transmission feel, and GR-FOUR AWD that lets the car rotate on throttle. Praise for the 8-speed DAT for daily-driver duty. Critiques include modest interior space and materials, mediocre fuel economy, and turbo-three NVH at idle.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners overwhelmingly praise GR-FOUR AWD, manual shifter feel, and the car's track-ready demeanor. Complaints involve fuel economy, cabin storage, and infotainment software.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen GR Supra (A90/J29), co-developed with BMW Z4 G29. 2026 is the final model year — MkV Final Edition celebrates the close of production. 2.0L 4-cyl trim discontinued; all 2026 cars use the 3.0L turbo I6.
models.model_summary: Toyota Gazoo Racing's two-seat sports coupe in its final model year. Three trims (3.0, 3.0 Premium, MkV Final Edition) all use the BMW B58-derived 3.0L twin-scroll-turbo inline-6 producing 382 hp and 368 lb-ft, with a 6-speed manual or 8-speed automatic and RWD. Final Edition is a limited 1,300-unit run for North America.

reliability.summary: Mature shared BMW B58 powertrain with strong reliability track record on both Toyota and BMW applications. Limited production volumes constrain dataset.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The GR Supra is low-volume and unlikely to receive a separate APEAL ranking even when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Enthusiast press treats the Final Edition as a sentimental sendoff for the BMW-sourced Supra. Praise for the B58 inline-six's power delivery, 6MT availability, and the Final Edition's tightened differential tuning and larger Brembo brakes. Critiques: tight cabin headroom for taller drivers, somewhat firm ride, and the philosophical complaint that it shares too much with the BMW Z4.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners love the engine character and tuner-community potential; gripes involve visibility, cabin space, and BMW-shared infotainment quirks.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The GR86 is low-volume and unlikely to receive a separate APEAL ranking even when 2026 APEAL is released.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners give very high ratings for the driving experience, manual-shifter feel and value. Complaints: cabin road noise, small back seat (rarely usable), modest cargo.

models.model_summary: Toyota's three-row, seven-passenger SUV is offered in three powertrain lines: 2.4L turbo gas (265 hp), 2.5L hybrid (245 hp combined), and Hybrid MAX (362 hp). EPA combined ranges from 22 MPG (ICE AWD) to 36 MPG (Hybrid FWD). Up to 97.5 cu ft of cargo with all seats folded.

reliability.summary: Two-plus model years into production; CR predicted reliability is average for the segment with isolated infotainment and electronics issues reported.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently call the Grand Highlander a roomy, family-friendly 3-row with genuinely usable third-row space and class-leading fuel economy in standard-hybrid form. The Hybrid MAX gets praise for 0-60 in the 6-second range and 5,000-lb towing; criticisms are an unrefined turbo-four note, conservative styling, and Platinum pricing approaching Lexus territory.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners highlight cargo space, third-row usability, comfortable ride, and (for hybrid) real-world fuel economy. Complaints: occasional infotainment freezes, turbo-four NVH on ICE trims, and Hybrid MAX's drop in MPG vs the standard hybrid.

models.generation_context: 250 series (J250) Land Cruiser, launched for US 2024 MY on the TNGA-F body-on-frame platform shared with Tacoma and 4Runner.
models.model_summary: The reborn Land Cruiser is a hybrid-only, full-time-4WD midsize SUV offered in two trims (heritage 1958 with round headlights and fabric seats; Land Cruiser with rectangular headlights and leather). Both use the 326-hp i-FORCE MAX 2.4L turbo hybrid with 8-speed AT, center and rear lockers standard, and 6,000-lb tow rating.

reliability.summary: Second model year of new 250 series platform; reliability still being established. Shared powertrain with 4Runner Hybrid and Tacoma i-FORCE MAX builds confidence; early-build infotainment quirks noted.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers welcome the Land Cruiser nameplate's US return as a more affordable midsize off-roader, praising the standard center and rear lockers, hybrid torque, and Toyota's heritage styling on 1958. Critiques include a small 5-passenger cabin (no 3rd row in the US 250 series), modest 23-MPG combined economy, and steel front bumper trim that limits aftermarket compatibility.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the off-road capability, hybrid torque and heritage 1958 styling; complaints involve infotainment glitches, smaller-than-expected cargo behind the rear seat, and lack of a third row.

models.generation_context: 2nd-gen Mirai (XS50), launched for 2021 MY on Toyota's GA-L rear-drive platform. 2026 reduces to a single XLE grade.
models.model_summary: The Mirai is Toyota's hydrogen fuel-cell sedan, sold in California only (where retail H2 fueling exists). For 2026 it consolidates to a single XLE grade with a 182-hp rear electric motor, three carbon-fiber hydrogen tanks, and an EPA-estimated 402-mile range. EPA MPGe is 74/70/72 (city/hwy/combined).

reliability.summary: Reliability data is thin owing to low volume and California-only sale; mechanically the Mirai's fuel-cell stack has performed well, but owners frequently cite hydrogen-station availability and downtime as the primary ownership pain point.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). JD Power does not publish APEAL for FCEV-only models due to low sample sizes; California owner sentiment is mixed and driven heavily by fueling logistics. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released though the Mirai is unlikely to receive a separate ranking.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers acknowledge the Mirai's quiet, smooth, near-luxury driving experience, RWD chassis composure, and standout 400+ mile range, but consistently warn buyers that California's hydrogen station network is small and unreliable, fuel prices have surged, and resale value is poor. Most reviews call it a fascinating engineering project that is hard to recommend as a primary car.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the cabin, ride and zero-emission driving, but recurring frustrations are hydrogen station outages, escalating H2 fuel costs, and poor resale value.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen Prius (XW60), launched for 2023 MY. 2026 standardizes BSM/RCTA and adds Nightshade trim on both hybrid and PHEV lines.
models.model_summary: Toyota's 5th-generation Prius is a sleek 4-door liftback offered in two parallel powertrain lines: a 194-hp/196-hp hybrid (FWD or AWD) and a 220-hp Plug-in Hybrid (FWD only) with 39–44 miles of EPA electric range. The hybrid hits 57 combined MPG on the base LE; the PHEV scores up to 127 MPGe combined.

reliability.summary: Prius has long been one of Toyota's most-reliable nameplates; 5th-gen platform now in its 4th model year is showing CR predicted-reliability scores at or near the top of the small-car class. JD Power VDS not yet published for 2026.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published 2026-specific APEAL; 5th-gen Prius generally rates above segment average for styling and emotional appeal.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rave about the 5th-gen Prius's transformed styling, sharp driving manners, and big fuel-economy gains. Praise for the PHEV's quick acceleration (220 hp), 44-mile EV range, and quiet cabin; critics knock cramped rear seating, low door sills, and small infotainment-screen ergonomics on lower trims. Most outlets recommend the PHEV SE as the value pick.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners overwhelmingly praise real-world fuel economy, styling, and quiet ride. Complaints: tight back seat for adults, sloping roofline that pinches rear headroom, and low-stance entry/exit.

models.generation_context: All-new 6th-gen RAV4 (XA60), launched for 2026 MY. Goes fully electrified — hybrid and PHEV only, no ICE-only variant. First-ever GR SPORT trim.
models.model_summary: Toyota's fully-redesigned 2026 RAV4 is offered exclusively as hybrid (226-236 hp) or plug-in hybrid (324 hp combined, up to 52 mi EV range). Twelve trims span three design themes — core (LE, XLE Premium, Limited), sport (SE, XSE, GR SPORT PHEV) and rugged (Woodland). EPA combined reaches 43 MPG on Hybrid LE FWD.

reliability.summary: All-new generation; reliability not yet established. Toyota's hybrid powertrain has historically been one of the most-reliable hybrids in the industry, which should carry over.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The all-new 2026 RAV4 has no APEAL history. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Press is broadly positive on the all-new RAV4: stronger hybrid output, cleaner design, more interior tech, and the new PHEV's 52-mile EV range. The first-ever GR SPORT PHEV adds enthusiast appeal. Reservations: the loss of pure ICE means higher base price, and the new chassis is heavier than the outgoing model.

owner_reviews.summary: Too few owner reviews available for the freshly launched 2026 generation; prior RAV4 hybrid owners consistently praised reliability and real-world fuel economy.

models.notes: All-new 2026 RAV4 is hybrid-only (no pure ICE). Two powertrain lines: hybrid (LE base + LE AWD/SE/XLE Premium/XSE/Limited/Woodland step-ups) and PHEV (SE base + XSE/Woodland/GR Sport step-ups). The PHEV's 22.7 kWh battery is up sharply from the prior 18.1 kWh, yielding up to 52 mi of EV range. Note that DC fast charging is reserved for upper PHEV trims (XSE, Woodland, GR Sport) — the SE PHEV gets only Level 1/2 AC. NHTSA and IIHS have not separately rated the all-new 2026 RAV4 as of research.

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen Sequoia (XK80), launched for 2023 MY on TNGA-F. Hybrid-only from launch. 2026 adds standard power-folding 3rd row and Platinum massaging seats.
models.model_summary: Toyota's full-size, body-on-frame 3-row SUV is hybrid-only — every trim uses the i-FORCE MAX 3.4L twin-turbo V6 hybrid for 437 hp and 583 lb-ft through a 10-speed automatic. Six trims (SR5, Limited, Platinum, 1794, TRD Pro, Capstone) tow up to 9,520 lb. RWD standard on SR5/Limited; 4WD standard on the rest.

reliability.summary: Three model years into the 3rd-gen platform; CR predicted reliability is mid-pack; some owners report turbocharger and infotainment niggles. Powertrain is shared with Tundra.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published 2026-specific APEAL for the Sequoia.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the hybrid V6's torque, towing capacity, and luxurious upper-trim cabins (Capstone, Platinum), but consistently flag the small 3rd-row cargo area (driven by the live rear axle and battery placement) and an unrefined ride compared with rivals like the Suburban. The Capstone is criticized as expensive for what amounts to fancy upholstery and badging.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners like the strong tow rating, hybrid powertrain torque, and resale; complaints are awkward 3rd-row cargo, rear-axle thumpy ride over expansion joints, and inflated upper-trim pricing.

trims.notes: Base trim of the (sole) i-FORCE MAX hybrid line. Power-folding 3rd row is new standard equipment for 2026 across the lineup.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen Sienna (XL40), launched for 2021 MY. Hybrid-only since launch. 2026 adds standard features across the lineup.
models.model_summary: Toyota's minivan has been hybrid-only since the 2021 redesign. A 2.5L four with electric motors produces 245 hp combined; FWD or electronic on-demand AWD. Six trims (LE, XLE, XSE, Woodland, Limited, Platinum) deliver EPA combined up to 36 MPG and a 3,500-lb tow rating.

reliability.summary: Mature platform with strong CR predicted reliability across recent years. Hybrid system is a proven Toyota architecture.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published 2026 APEAL; prior years are average for the minivan class.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rate the Sienna a top family-minivan pick for its 36-MPG hybrid economy, available AWD (unique to the class), and proven reliability. Critiques: modest 245-hp acceleration when fully loaded, less interior plastics polish than the Honda Odyssey, and some early infotainment software annoyances.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise fuel economy, available AWD, and resale; complaints include modest power, road noise, and infotainment quirks.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen Tacoma, launched for 2024 MY on TNGA-F. Shares architecture with new 4Runner and Land Cruiser. i-FORCE MAX hybrid new for this generation.
models.model_summary: Toyota's midsize pickup is offered with two powertrains: i-FORCE (2.4L turbo gas, 228 or 278 hp) and i-FORCE MAX (2.4L turbo hybrid, 326 hp / 465 lb-ft). Eight trims range from work-truck SR to overlanding Trailhunter and TRD Pro, all built on the new TNGA-F platform with available rear-coil suspension. Max tow rating is 6,500 lb.

reliability.summary: Two model years into the all-new 4th-gen platform; CR predicted reliability is currently below average due to recall and software issues reported in early builds. Toyota has addressed several through recalls.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Press is broadly favorable on the new Tacoma's modern cabin, optional rear-coil suspension and strong off-road bona fides on TRD trims; Trailhunter and TRD Pro draw the most enthusiast attention. Critiques include turbo-four NVH compared with the outgoing V6, firm IsoDynamic Performance Seat ergonomics on TRD Pro, and steep pricing at the top.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners like the off-road capability, hybrid torque on i-FORCE MAX, and modern interior. Complaints involve early-build infotainment issues, turbo-four NVH, and rough on-road ride on the more off-road-tuned trims.

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen Tundra (XK70), launched for 2022 MY on TNGA-F. i-FORCE 3.4L twin-turbo V6 replaced the 5.7L V8; i-FORCE MAX hybrid offered alongside.
models.model_summary: Toyota's full-size pickup uses two powertrains: i-FORCE 3.4L twin-turbo V6 (358 hp / 406 lb-ft) and i-FORCE MAX hybrid (437 hp / 583 lb-ft). Seven trims (SR, SR5, Limited, Platinum, 1794, TRD Pro, Capstone) sit on a coil-spring rear suspension; max tow is up to 12,000 lb with i-FORCE.

reliability.summary: Five model years into the 3rd-gen platform; recent CR scores improved after early-gen turbocharger and wastegate recalls were addressed.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power APEAL not yet published for 2026.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers like the strong twin-turbo V6 / hybrid powertrain torque, modern multimedia, and Capstone's luxury cabin. Critiques focus on real-world fuel economy that lags rivals, a noisy turbo-V6 note, and Hybrid MPG that doesn't beat ICE meaningfully unless lightly loaded.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise torque, ride quality from the coil-sprung rear, and Capstone luxury. Complaints involve poorer-than-expected highway MPG, transmission shift behavior in some scenarios, and turbo lag.
